## Contract

**Delegator use only.** Any agent that launches subagents reads this registry to select relevant skills, then passes exact `SKILL.md` paths for the subagent to read before work.

`SKILL.md` is the source of truth. Do not inject summaries by default — pass paths so subagents load the full runtime contract.

## Compact rules

### branch-pr
- Every PR MUST link an approved issue with `status:approved` label and close keyword
- Exactly one `type:*` label per PR (bug, feature, docs, refactor, chore, breaking-change)
- Branch names must match `^(feat|fix|chore|docs|style|refactor|perf|test|build|ci|revert)\/[a-z0-9._-]+$`
- PR body must include linked issue, PR type, summary, changes table, and test plan
- Automated checks must pass before merge; shellcheck on modified scripts
- Blank PRs without issue linkage are blocked by GitHub Actions

### chained-pr
- Split PRs over 400 changed lines unless maintainer accepts `size:exception`
- Keep each PR reviewable in ≤60 minutes; keep tests/docs with the unit they verify
- State start/end, dependencies, follow-up, and out-of-scope items in every chained PR
- Child PRs must include a dependency diagram marking the current PR with 📍
- Feature Branch Chain: draft/no-merge tracker PR, children target the tracker branch
- Do not mix chain strategies after the user chooses one

### cognitive-doc-design
- Lead with the answer: decision/action first, context after
- Progressive disclosure: happy path → details → edge cases → references
- Chunk related info into small sections; use tables, checklists, templates
- Signpost with headings, callouts, summaries for scannability
- Default structure: outcome title, one-paragraph context, quick path, details table, checklist, next step

### comment-writer
- Start with the actionable point; do not recap the whole PR first
- Be warm and direct like a thoughtful teammate, not a bot
- Keep to 1-3 short paragraphs or a tight bullet list
- Explain WHY when asking for a change (technical reason)
- Comment on highest-value issue, not every tiny preference
- Match thread language; Spanish → Rioplatense voseo (podés, tenés)
- No em dashes — use commas, periods, or parentheses

### issue-creation
- Blank issues disabled; MUST use bug_report.yml or feature_request.yml
- Every issue gets `status:needs-review` on creation; maintainer must add `status:approved` before PR
- Questions go to Discussions, not issues
- Bug report: pre-flight checks, description, steps, expected/actual behavior, OS, agent, shell
- Feature request: pre-flight checks, problem, proposed solution, alternatives, additional context
- Search for duplicates before creating any issue

### judgment-day
- Resolve project skills before launching judges: read registry, match compact rules by target
- Launch two blind judges in parallel; never review code yourself
- Classify warnings as `WARNING (real)` only if normal intended use triggers them
- Ask before fixing Round 1 confirmed issues; re-judge after fixes
- Terminal states: JUDGMENT: APPROVED or JUDGMENT: ESCALATED
- After 2 fix iterations with remaining issues, ask user whether to continue

### go-testing
- Prefer table-driven tests with `t.Run(tt.name, ...)`
- Test behavior and state transitions, not implementation trivia
- Use `t.TempDir()` for filesystem tests; never rely on real home directory
- Integration tests skippable with `testing.Short()` when running external commands
- Golden files must be deterministic; update only through repo's `-update` path
- Use small mocks/interfaces around system or command execution boundaries

### skill-creator
- Skill is a runtime instruction contract for LLM, not human documentation
- Required sections: Activation Contract, Hard Rules, Decision Gates, Execution Steps, Output Contract, References
- `description` MUST be one physical quoted line ≤250 chars with trigger words first
- Frontmatter requires name, description, license, metadata.author, metadata.version
- Keep body 180-450 tokens; move examples/schemas/background to `assets/` or `references/`
- References must be local files relative to skill directory

### work-unit-commits
- Commit by work unit (deliverable behavior), not by file type
- Keep tests with the code they verify; keep docs with the user-visible change
- Each commit should be a candidate chained PR if the change grows
- If SDD tasks forecast >400 lines, group commits into chained PR slices before implementation
- Before committing: one clear purpose, repo still works, rollback is reasonable
- Use Conventional Commits: `feat(auth): description`

## Loading protocol

1. Match task context and target files against the `Trigger / description` column
2. Pass only matching `Path` values to subagent under `## Skills to load before work`
3. Instruct subagent to read exact `SKILL.md` files before reading/writing/reviewing/testing
4. If no matching skill exists, proceed without project skill injection and report `skill_resolution: none`

## Project conventions

- No `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`, or `copilot-instructions.md` found in gym-backend
- Parent `../.atl/skill-registry.md` has comprehensive user skill listing (includes Codex system skills)
